frameworks.name: cognitive science of human-machine interaction
frameworks.one_line: framework linking levels of description to study brain mechanisms of human-robot interaction

frameworks.name: social cognition
frameworks.one_line: social cognitive systems extended to interactions with machines and robots

frameworks.name: cognitive neuroscience
frameworks.one_line: multi-level brain and behavioural methods leveraged for human-machine research

core_question: What framework can integrate cognitive and brain mechanisms supporting human interactions with increasingly social machines?
key_assumptions: machines are taking on increasingly social roles in everyday environments
human-machine interactions recruit multiple cognitive and brain systems
different levels of description can be linked via cognitive neuroscience theory and methods
individuals' expectations and prior experiences shape interaction
interpretations: a multi-level framework can respect diversity of machines, individuals, and brain systems
human-machine interaction poses unique challenges and opportunities for brain and behavioural science
